archimate:ApplicationInterface.name: eQualification
archimate:ApplicationInterface.documentation:
Definition: The eQualification Application Service refers to a digital service that enables the management and processing of qualifications and certifications in an electronic format. It facilitates the creation, verification, storage, and sharing of qualifications and related documentation electronically. The service provides a secure and efficient platform for individuals, educational institutions, employers, and certification bodies to manage qualifications, track progress, and validate credentials. It may include features such as online qualification submission, document verification, real-time qualification status updates, and integration with educational databases and certification authorities.
Source: European Commission
Source reference: https://ec.europa.eu/education/sites/education/files/2014-11-25-equal-recognition-foreign-qualifications_en.pdf
LOST view: TVA-API Enablers [Motivation]
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/egovera/EQualificationApplicationInterface
ABB name: egovera:EQualificationApplicationInterface
EIRA concept: eira:ArchitectureBuildingBlock
Last modification: 2023-11-20
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/egovera/EQualificationApplicationInterface
Interoperability Layer: TechnicalApplication
Specialises: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/WebServiceApplicationInterface

properties.value: The eQualification Application Service refers to a digital service that enables the management and processing of qualifications and certifications in an electronic format. It facilitates the creation, verification, storage, and sharing of qualifications and related documentation electronically. The service provides a secure and efficient platform for individuals, educational institutions, employers, and certification bodies to manage qualifications, track progress, and validate credentials. It may include features such as online qualification submission, document verification, real-time qualification status updates, and integration with educational databases and certification authorities.
